122189.explanation: Every party should nominate aldermen based on conviction whom they consider suitable for the role of alderman.

122190.explanation: Municipal elections are held every four years, and all residents can express their political preferences. When all important issues are decided directly by referendum, this undermines the authority of the democratically elected municipal council.

122193.explanation: It must remain possible to operate a mini campsite or sales stall on an agricultural business. This helps farmers earn extra income alongside their main activity and makes our rural area more attractive.

122194.explanation: Generating sustainable energy is often a profit model or cost-saving activity. There should be no financial support from the municipality for that. If generating sustainable energy serves the general interest, subsidy options are available from the national government.

122195.explanation: We do not want to bring distribution centers to Reimerswaal. They take up a lot of space and provide little employment. Expansion of existing distribution centers, for example for the shellfish sector, should be possible.

122197.explanation: Where possible, we are positive about locating migrant workers on-site at agricultural businesses.

122199.explanation: Banning open fireplaces is patronizing. The SGP does not want to go down that path. But there must be strict supervision of their proper use. Especially in residential areas, they can cause nuisance.

122201.explanation: SGP Reimerswaal does not see setting off fireworks as a government task. Moreover, setting off fireworks conflicts with how God asks us to care for creation.

122202.explanation: All residents can bring their bulky waste to the recycling center for free. Collecting bulky waste at home costs money. It is therefore logical that the municipality charges a fee for this.

122203.explanation: The SGP wants much stricter noise standards at parties and festivals. To prevent nuisance for local residents, but also to protect the hearing of visitors to these events.

122206.explanation: Sporting and social activities are important for everyone. The municipality of Reimerswaal is currently affiliated with the sports and culture fund for residents with low incomes who cannot afford a sports club. SGP Reimerswaal wants to continue this affiliation with the sports and culture fund.

122207.explanation: Local broadcasters should primarily focus on delivering local news. If they reduce their sports coverage, sufficient funding will be available.

122210.explanation: Sports clubs are important, but the primary responsibility for their functioning lies with the club itself. If there is insufficient community support to maintain the club, the municipality cannot continue to contribute community funds.

122213.explanation: Safety and enforcement are important. But at the moment, there are no problems that justify expanding the capacity of special enforcement officers (BOA).

122214.explanation: At certain locations where there is structural nuisance or vandalism, camera surveillance is a justified measure.

122219.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation and not for permanent residence. In Reimerswaal, there are few holiday homes and this is not currently a big problem. In other parts of the country, there are significant problems on holiday parks where permanent residence also occurs.

122220.explanation: To meet the demand for new homes, expansion outside the villages is needed in addition to housing construction within the villages.

122221.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits often do so for good reason. There is a cause why they have no work, even in today’s society with many vacancies. When it is possible to work, SGP Reimerswaal supports this principle.

122222.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes should only be considered as a last resort but should not be abolished. It can prevent debt problems and many other issues.

122223.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in society. At the same time, the balance must be maintained so that working remains worthwhile and no one is left out.

122226.explanation: Currently, the maximum speed in all residential areas is 30 km/h. There is no reason to increase this.

122227.explanation: The SGP considers it very important that people can receive care from providers that align with their religious beliefs.

122228.explanation: Youth care is primarily about providing good care. We are convinced that good care also prevents high costs in the future.

122236.explanation: Podium Reimerswaal hosts performances that conflict with God’s good commandments for society. SGP Reimerswaal does not want municipal support for such performances.

122239.explanation: Social rental housing should be built as much as needed. We should not focus solely on a number.

122240.explanation: High residential apartment buildings do not fit with the rural character of Reimerswaal.

122242.explanation: All villages have a center with their own facilities. That should remain this way.

122243.explanation: There must be sufficient parking space everywhere.

122245.explanation: Fireworks nuisance is a major annoyance for many residents. This must be tackled firmly.

122247.explanation: Sunday is a special day. The SGP finds it important that this resurrection day of Christ remains unique. It is a great privilege to have one day for church attendance, listening to the Word of God, and mutual fellowship. We will continue to advocate for this.

122189.explanation: Selection should be based on the quality of the person.

122190.explanation: Residents elect their representatives and must be able to trust them.

122193.explanation: Expanding camper sites to 35 places and a small sales stall keeps the countryside livable.

122195.explanation: Expansion of local businesses is our first priority.

122197.explanation: We do not want housing for temporary or seasonally-oriented labor migrants in the village centers.

122201.explanation: A stall selling traditional Dutch doughnuts (oliebollen) should not be missing there.

122202.explanation: That is what the recycling center is for.

122203.explanation: These must comply with legal requirements.

122206.explanation: Existing scheme for special social assistance.

122236.explanation: Podium Reimerswaal must be financially self-sustaining.

122240.explanation: They must be affordable apartments. Space is scarce, so building upwards is necessary.

122242.explanation: For us, yes, but it must be or become profitable for a business owner.

122243.explanation: There are more and more cars, and public transport is not available everywhere in the villages.

122189.explanation: The municipality decides itself. The Municipalities Act does not impose requirements. Selection is based on a profile and proven suitability.

122190.explanation: The municipal council may decide whether or not to offer a referendum.

122193.explanation: Dependent on the Environment Act and zoning/environmental plan.

122194.explanation: There is no legal obligation for this. However, it might be possible to apply for the national ISDE subsidy or the local municipality could be willing to provide financial support.

122195.explanation: A municipality can refuse provided this is included in the environmental plan.

122199.explanation: There is no national law banning wood burning. Municipalities may impose restrictions or bans themselves if necessary or desirable.

122201.explanation: A municipality may organize a fireworks display. However, it is not a legal obligation.

122202.explanation: The national government has established that bulky waste can be collected by the municipality or brought to a recycling center. Nowhere does it state this must be free of charge.

122203.explanation: The national government has determined that municipalities may set their own noise limits for events, which the municipality has included in its Environmental Plan.

122206.explanation: Municipalities can choose to provide financial support through, for example, Special Assistance.

122207.explanation: Municipalities are not legally required to provide extra funding to the local broadcaster.

122210.explanation: Municipalities often own sports halls, fields, and swimming pools. They must manage and maintain these, but that does not mean they have to financially rescue associations.

122213.explanation: The municipality has a duty of care for public order and safety, supported by the police, the mayor, and enforcement officers. The municipal council decides (policy choice) the number of enforcement officers based on nuisance, livability, supervision and enforcement of municipal regulations, and police capacity.

122214.explanation: No legal obligation exists, but it is allowed under strict conditions (Municipalities Act art 151c).

122219.explanation: Although there is no legal obligation, our faction would welcome this considering the existing housing shortage.

122220.explanation: It is the province that decides this, so respecting this provincial policy is a political choice.

122221.explanation: Municipalities may ask for it if it fits someone's situation, but it is not a general obligation for everyone.

122222.explanation: Fee waivers are a political choice, not a legal obligation or a legal prohibition. Our party wants to support every person who cannot pay according to the applicable rules.

122223.explanation: Municipalities determine their own poverty policies, including income limits (e.g., 110%, 120%, 130% of the social minimum). We fully support this and prefer even more measures to combat poverty.

122226.explanation: Reimerswaal has 30 km/h zones in residential areas and main roads with 50 km/h. We consider this a good arrangement balancing safety and economic interests.

122227.explanation: Care recipients may choose a provider that fits their faith, culture, or beliefs-as long as that provider is available and complies with the law.

122228.explanation: Price is one factor, but never the most important.

122236.explanation: Podium Reimerswaal is an independent foundation; the municipality may not interfere with its board or organization. The municipality may encourage innovation through subsidy conditions, performance agreements, and cultural policy. Innovation is therefore a political choice in the interest of all our residents (young and old).

122239.explanation: The new Housing Supervision Act (Regiewet) mandates that an average of 30% of all new construction must be social rental housing. We fully support this.

122240.explanation: Preferably no tall residential buildings to preserve the rural character of the villages. We do support 'a residential building with two floors, consisting of apartments on the ground and first floors.' This results in more housing per sqm.

122242.explanation: Reimerswaal has villages with amenities, but not every village has its own village center-and it was never designed that way.

122243.explanation: The municipality must adhere to its own parking standards with new developments.

122245.explanation: The policy already aims to prevent nuisance based on past experiences. We advocate for no stricter measures but more preventive actions together with parents, schools, churches, and associations to prevent nuisance.

122247.explanation: The municipal council determines this via the Shop Opening Hours ordinance. It is therefore a political choice and not a 'right' of supermarkets.

122189.explanation: Equality and balance between men and women should be pursued. Men and women must have equal opportunities to hold administrative positions. A board does not have to be exactly 50/50 men and women.

122190.explanation: We are positive about citizen input and consultation. Our preference is to strengthen representative democracy and other forms of resident participation.

122193.explanation: Not allowed without limits. Only permitted if it remains small-scale, does not cause nuisance, local amenities are not displaced, and nature and landscape are respected.

122194.explanation: We invest in clean energy and thereby also in green neighborhoods. Climate policy is social policy; therefore, we center climate justice. Cooperation should be rewarded.

122195.explanation: Distribution centers are often not sustainable. Municipalities focusing on the local economy and stimulating small-scale businesses encourage shorter supply chains and reduce CO2 emissions.

122197.explanation: Employers are responsible for the housing of labor migrants under strict conditions. Labor migrants must be protected against exploitation. This means strict rules and supervision. Housing must be independent of the employment contract (not immediately homeless upon dismissal).

122199.explanation: Wood smoke contains fine particles and other toxic substances that can penetrate deep into the lungs. These substances can cause respiratory complaints. Burning wood emits CO2 and other greenhouse gases.

122201.explanation: The ban on illegal fireworks is intended to reduce safety risks and nuisance, not to guarantee a fireworks display. It is not legally mandatory, so our municipality must have the freedom to choose based on budget and preferences.

122202.explanation: The municipality must have a well-organized waste policy, where bulky waste is collected on fixed days and in an accessible way. Clear communication to residents to combat littering and dumping.

122203.explanation: Strict noise standards do not mean 'ban everything,' but rather ensure a healthy balance between business activity, events, and living. The municipal regulations provide clear rules on this.

122206.explanation: Reimerswaal participates in both the adult sports & culture fund and the youth sports & culture fund. We believe everyone should benefit from the power of sports and culture.

122207.explanation: The local broadcaster ensures independent local news coverage. Without sufficient funding, that journalistic function cannot be properly performed.

122210.explanation: Financial support for sports clubs is not a gift but an investment in health, social cohesion, and equal opportunities. It yields more for society than it costs.

122213.explanation: Enforcement officers must be visibly present to promote safety and livability and enforce rules where necessary to be effective and build trust in the government. Sufficient enforcement officers are therefore necessary.

122214.explanation: If demonstrably necessary, such as at stations and for (firework) nuisance, carefully limited and with serious privacy protection, it can be a meaningful addition. We consider prevention and visible enforcement at least as important.

122219.explanation: We do not support general legalization; only in exceptional cases and with sufficient adjustments can it be considered. Allowing this does not offer a sustainable solution to the housing shortage.

122220.explanation: We choose infill development, restructuring, and smart upward building instead of expanding outward. Nature areas and agricultural land are protected, which reduces clutter in the rural area.

122221.explanation: Social assistance is a right to income, not a punishment. Voluntary, supported participation can provide benefits for both the individual and society.

122222.explanation: It is a legal, social, and moral obligation to protect vulnerable citizens and ensure equal treatment. We support debt prevention through early advice and guidance.

122223.explanation: Many households just above the current threshold face difficulties with rent, energy, and groceries. Raising the threshold helps these families before the situation worsens.

122226.explanation: Slower traffic makes streets safer for everyone. In residential areas with intersections and crossings (schools), 50 km/h is often unrealistic. 30 km/h gives people more space.

122227.explanation: Healthcare is a basic right. Identity-based care can lead to exclusion of people who do not share that identity. We want a society without dividing lines, equal for everyone, personal where necessary.

122228.explanation: Youth care requires quality, trust, and continuity. A system that chooses primarily based on price does not do justice to this.

122236.explanation: Podium Reimerswaal has proven for many years to be valuable to the Reimerswaal community. Podium Reimerswaal involves people in society and stimulates talent development. Supporting valuable institutions yields more than it costs.

122239.explanation: We want to counter segregation. Social housing ensures that people with lower incomes can also live affordably. By building mixed neighborhoods, a social mix arises, keeping neighborhoods attractive and livable.

122240.explanation: High-rise buildings make it possible to build more homes without affecting the countryside. They help combat housing shortages and long waiting lists.

122242.explanation: A village center is the heart of a village where people can shop and meet. It is a place that forms the social, economic, and cultural core of a village.

122243.explanation: Too many parking spaces make car use attractive; we want to promote sustainable mobility. Parking spaces take up a lot of space that could otherwise be used for greenery or playgrounds.

122245.explanation: We try to prevent nuisance as much as possible. Illegal fireworks sales are also a major problem in Reimerswaal and require strict enforcement by the municipality and government.

122247.explanation: Supermarkets may basically decide for themselves about Sunday openings, as long as they comply with local rules and consider staff and surroundings.

122189.explanation: The VVD considers it important that the executive board is a good reflection of the population, so an equal number of men and women.

122190.explanation: The VVD believes it is important that residents also have a say in important issues, and a referendum is a good tool for that.

122193.explanation: A well-considered assessment must be made based on location and possibilities. So not 'always' but carefully considered.

122194.explanation: When there are initiatives for sustainable energy generation, this does not unconditionally mean that the municipality must financially contribute. The municipality can contribute financially only if it also adds value to the community.

122195.explanation: The municipality should not simply prohibit the establishment of distribution centers but must make a well-considered decision when an application for establishment is submitted.

122199.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves cause a lot of fine particles and sometimes nuisance. The government must set technical requirements for wood stoves and fireplaces so that this nuisance is greatly reduced.

122201.explanation: If a fireworks show would reduce the nuisance of fireworks, it is certainly a good initiative. However, the VVD believes this will not change the nuisance and then it is a waste of taxpayers’ money that does not achieve the intended goal.

122202.explanation: This ensures that people who cannot bring bulky waste themselves can dispose of it properly and do not dump it somewhere illegally.

122203.explanation: The noise standards are good; they just need to be enforced.

122206.explanation: For people who cannot afford it themselves, there should be opportunities to participate in sports, and the municipality/government should take charge of this. Automatically paying membership fees for a sports club without conditions is one step too far for the VVD.

122207.explanation: Local media ensure that people are well informed about their own living environment and local government. This contributes to a stronger democracy.

122210.explanation: If a sports club is in trouble, there should be an option for the municipality to provide support. But according to rules so everyone receives equal treatment.

122213.explanation: There are enough enforcement officers and safety is well arranged. Only on exceptional days we may have too few officers, such as Mussel Day and in December, but then we can always call on a neighboring municipality.

122219.explanation: Vacation homes are basically not intended for permanent residence, but if there is no alternative, the VVD believes that vacation homes may also temporarily be used as a permanent address.

122220.explanation: Wherever there are opportunities to build, they must be utilized. We have a housing shortage, and we must do everything to solve this as quickly as possible.

122221.explanation: Everyone should contribute to society as much as they can. And if there are opportunities to be useful in society, people receiving social assistance should also contribute.

122222.explanation: When it cannot be helped because people cannot get out of debt otherwise, waiving debts is better than pressuring them. This may prevent more serious problems. But debt waivers should not be stopped outright; this should be reviewed on a case-by-case basis.

122223.explanation: This is more the responsibility of the national government. They must set the rules.

122227.explanation: People who need care should receive it with respect for their culture, religion, or beliefs. But preferably no special care because that is polarizing.

122228.explanation: Price is not the most important; it is better to closely monitor the quality of youth care and its effectiveness.

122236.explanation: The VVD believes that funding must again be made available for Podium Reimerswaal. Podium Reimerswaal meets [incomplete]

122239.explanation: We have a housing vision that describes how many social rental homes are needed. That housing vision must simply be followed because it is based on calculations of the population composition.

122240.explanation: Reimerswaal is a municipality where many high residential towers do not fit. The towers that do exist are fine, and if more would fit in certain centers, it should be investigated whether that is possible.

122243.explanation: In some places, additional parking spaces are needed, but this must not come at the expense of livability and greenery in the centers.

122245.explanation: In Reimerswaal, in the VVD’s opinion, everything is already being done to limit fireworks nuisance during New Year’s Eve as much as possible. Stricter measures may cause even more resistance and reaction.

122247.explanation: In neighboring municipalities, supermarkets are also already open. The VVD believes that the entrepreneur should decide this themselves, not the government.

122189.explanation: It's about the best candidates. Not about gender.

122190.explanation: Through Reimerswaal Praat Mee, we take the opinions of our residents seriously.

122193.explanation: Of course, only with local products and not too large.

122195.explanation: Our municipality is not suitable for that.

122197.explanation: This can provide more available housing in the villages for the local residents.

122201.explanation: We must make it possible for residents to apply for a permit for this.

122202.explanation: We have the recycling center for this.

122203.explanation: Residents should also be able to celebrate a party.

122207.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives a subsidy.

122210.explanation: This can also be done by reducing the rent.

122213.explanation: Safety is important and more enforcement officers (BOA’s) are needed for that.

122219.explanation: We prefer to focus on a sustainable solution by building affordable homes.

122220.explanation: First, see if it can be done within the village itself, and then at the edges of the villages.

122222.explanation: People who really cannot afford it must continue to be able to use a waiver scheme.

122223.explanation: We must do everything possible to ensure that residents do not live below the poverty line. We can do this by keeping municipal levies as low as possible.

122226.explanation: As long as it is not a through road, the maximum speed may be 30 km/h.

122227.explanation: We strive for the best care for our residents.

122228.explanation: The most important thing is that a youth care organization is the best for our young people.

122236.explanation: The CDA believes that Podium Reimerswaal may receive a subsidy.

122239.explanation: At least 30%! Even if this means that project developers make less profit on a project.

122240.explanation: This does not fit in our municipality.

122242.explanation: A community center connects, unites, and builds a strong society.

122243.explanation: There are usually enough parking spaces now. Cycling to the village is also an option.

122245.explanation: Focus on prevention and providing information to young people.

122247.explanation: In our municipality, the Sunday rest as we currently have it is appropriate:

statements.title: Reimerswaal should take stricter measures against fireworks nuisance during New Year's Eve.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Reimerswaal.

titleParts.text: In Reimerswaal moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The residents of Reimerswaal should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Reimerswaal moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Reimerswaal should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more camera surveillance in Reimerswaal.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be permitted in Reimerswaal.
The municipality should renovate Podium Reimerswaal.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Reimerswaal should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, currently 28% of homes are social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Reimerswaal moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: In Reimerswaal all supermarkets should be able to decide for themselves whether to open on Sundays.
